destination_overview.best_time_to_visit: Early April is the transition from winter to spring. While the snow begins to melt, the 'Astana Wind' is still prevalent. It is a great time for indoor luxury experiences, KHL hockey playoffs, and cultural events.

recommended_places.name: Barys Arena
recommended_places.description: The home of the Barys Astana KHL hockey team. A world-class multi-purpose indoor arena.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Directly matches the user's request to 'see barys'. April is typically KHL playoff season.
tips: Book VIP lounge tickets for a luxury experience
Visit the official fan shop for authentic jerseys

recommended_places.name: Zhaksylyk Ushkempirov Martial Arts Palace
recommended_places.description: The premier venue for martial arts in Kazakhstan, including elite judo training facilities.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Matches the request to see the 'biggest judo training halls'. It is the hub for combat sports in the capital.
tips: Check for national judo team training times
The architecture is stunning for photography

recommended_places.name: Qazaq Gourmet
recommended_places.description: High-end restaurant specializing in refined, modern interpretations of traditional Kazakh cuisine.
recommended_places.why_recommended: The best place to eat Kazakh meals in a luxury setting, perfect for students of SDU who appreciate their culture.
tips: Try the Beshbarmak with horse meat
The interior is museum-quality, great for content creation

recommended_places.name: Burabay National Park (Mountains)
recommended_places.address: Burabay, Akmola Region (2.5 hours from Astana)
recommended_places.description: Known as the 'Pearl of Kazakhstan', this park features mountains, pine forests, and lakes.
recommended_places.why_recommended: The nearest 'mountain' experience to Astana. A luxury trip requires a private transfer here.
tips: Hire a private guide for the 'Bolektau' hike
Stay at Rixos Borovoe for ultimate luxury

recommended_places.name: Sky Beach Club (Khan Shatyr)
recommended_places.address: Turan Ave 37, Khan Shatyr Entertainment Center
recommended_places.description: An indoor tropical beach with sand from the Maldives and a constant 35°C temperature.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Provides 'Beach Time' in Astana's April climate inside an architectural marvel designed by Norman Foster.
tips: Rent a private cabana
Go on a weekday to avoid crowds

recommended_places.name: The Ritz-Carlton Spa, Astana
recommended_places.description: Ultimate luxury spa offering panoramic city views and high-end wellness treatments.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Matches the luxury travel style and interest in wellness perfectly.
tips: Try the signature salt scrub
The relaxation room overlooks the Baiterek Tower

recommended_places.name: Baiterek Tower
recommended_places.description: An iconic monument symbolizing the golden egg of the mythical Samruk bird.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Must-see attraction for any first-time or returning visitor to the capital.
tips: Place your hand in the president's golden handprint
Go at sunset for the best lighting

recommended_places.name: Astana Opera
recommended_places.description: One of the most beautiful opera houses in the world with neoclassical architecture.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Represents the pinnacle of culture and luxury in the city.
tips: Dress formally to match the local elite
Book a private box for the best experience

recommended_places.name: Tary Coffee & Traditional Snacks
recommended_places.description: A stylish cafe focused on forgotten Kazakh national snacks and grains (Tary/Zhent).
recommended_places.why_recommended: Offers a cultural yet modern culinary experience that is highly 'Instagrammable'.
tips: Try the coffee with zhent
Buy packaged traditional snacks as souvenirs

recommended_places.name: Nur Alem Future Energy Museum (Sphere)
recommended_places.description: The largest spherical building in the world, legacy of EXPO-2017.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Unique architecture and educational exhibits on future wellness and energy.
tips: The glass walkway on the top floor is a must-do
The surrounding park is great for evening walks

recommended_places.name: National Museum of the Republic of Kazakhstan
recommended_places.description: The largest museum in Central Asia, housing the 'Golden Man'.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Essential for understanding the nomadic history and culture of Kazakhstan.
tips: Don't miss the Hall of Gold
The flying eagle light show in the lobby happens every hour

recommended_places.name: Selfie Restaurant Astana
recommended_places.description: A high-end restaurant from the White Rabbit Family, offering incredible views of the city.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Combines luxury dining with the best photographic angles of Astana's skyline.
tips: Request a window seat in advance
The cocktail menu is inventive and highly visual

recommended_places.name: Hazrat Sultan Mosque
recommended_places.description: The second largest mosque in Central Asia, an architectural masterpiece.
recommended_places.why_recommended: A stunning cultural site that offers a peaceful atmosphere for relaxation/reflection.
tips: Dress modestly (robes provided at entrance)
Visit at night when it is beautifully illuminated

recommended_places.name: Ailand (Duman) Aquarium
recommended_places.description: The only aquarium in the world located over 3000km from the ocean.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Unique 'ocean' experience in the heart of the steppe.
tips: Walk through the underwater tunnel
Check the schedule for shark feeding

recommended_places.name: Satti Restaurant
recommended_places.description: Traditional Kazakh food served in a grand, regal atmosphere.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Authentic Kazakh dining experience near the Baiterek tower.
tips: Try the 'Kuyrdak'
Perfect for group dinners with traditional music

recommended_places.name: Central Park (Stolichny Park)
recommended_places.description: The main city park, great for leisure and seeing the Ishim River.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Ideal for a slow luxury pace and fresh air between museum visits.
tips: Rent a bike or electric scooter
The embankment is perfect for sunset photos

recommended_places.name: Iridium Spa at St. Regis
recommended_places.description: Exclusive spa featuring a luxury pool, hammam, and bespoke treatments.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Fits the luxury lifestyle of the travelers and provides world-class relaxation.
tips: Book the 'Iridium Journey' treatment
Use the heated indoor pool

recommended_places.name: Keruen City Shopping Mall
recommended_places.description: One of the premier luxury shopping destinations in the city.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Ideal for purchasing luxury brands and seeing modern Kazakh retail culture.
tips: The food court has high-end options
Excellent Wi-Fi for posting content

recommended_places.name: Presidential Center of Culture
recommended_places.description: A yurt-shaped building containing historical artifacts and cultural exhibits.
recommended_places.why_recommended: Unique architecture and deep cultural insights for the teacher and student travelers.
tips: Great for architecture photography from the outside
Often hosts temporary art exhibitions

recommended_places.name: Mokki Restaurant
recommended_places.description: A modern 'market-style' luxury dining experience with various live cooking stations.
recommended_places.why_recommended: High-quality international and local food in a chic setting.
tips: The Sunday Brunch is the best in the city
Great for business-casual meetings

hidden_gems.name: Tary Cafe
hidden_gems.why_special: Modernizes traditional nomadic grains into trendy coffee and snacks

practical_information.getting_around: With a luxury budget, hire a private Chauffeur/Car Service (e.g., Executive Class Mercedes). Taxis via the Yandex Go app (Comfort+ or Business class) are also very reliable.
practical_information.local_currency: Kazakhstani Tenge (KZT). Credit cards are accepted everywhere in Astana.
language_tips: How much does this cost? (Bul qansha turady?)
cultural_notes: Respect the elders; it's a core part of Kazakh culture.
Take off your shoes when entering a home or certain traditional areas.
Astana is very safe, but dress warmly as April weather is unpredictable.
